docs.title: Welcome to MkDocs
docs.text: For full documentation visit mkdocs.org.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: 🛠️ Installation Guide
docs.text: This guide provides step-by-step instructions for setting up GENIUS-LLM. We focus on ensuring a robust installation process across various environments, with a particular emphasis on Linux-based High-Performance Computing (HPC) and Windows (WSL2/Native) systems.

docs.title: 1. Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before starting, please ensure your system meets the following requirements to ensure stability:

  • Operating System: Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+, CentOS 7+), macOS, or Windows 10/11.
  • Python Version: 3.8, 3.9, or 3.10 (Tested for stability with asynchronous LLM calls).
  • Database: MongoDB (v4.4 or higher) must be available as the backend for gene annotation storage.

docs.title: 2. Environment Management (Recommended)
docs.text:
To avoid library version conflicts and ensure reproducibility, we strongly recommend using a Conda virtual environment.

# Create a clean environment
conda create -n genius_llm python=3.9 -y

# Activate the environment
conda activate genius_llm

docs.title: 3. Installation
docs.text:
To install GENIUS-LLM, follow these steps: 

# Navigate to the project root
cd 20260410GENIUS-LLM

# Install in editable mode with all dependencies
pip install -e .

docs.title: 4. 数据库设置与初始化
docs.text:
GENIUS-LLM 利用 MongoDB 进行高速基因数据检索。服务必须处于激活状态，才能运行预测。

🔹 Linux（systemd）：

1.对于拥有 root 权限（sudo）的用户 

sudo systemctl start mongod
# Verify status
sudo systemctl status mongod

2.对于没有 root 权限的用户 

# Create a local data directory
mkdir -p ~/mongodb_data
# Launch MongoDB locally on the default port
mongod --dbpath ~/mongodb_data --port 27017 --fork --logpath ~/mongodb_data/mongodb.log
🔹 对于Windows的用户： win+R ，输入services.msc，右键启动MongoDB Server (MongoDB)。

"Everything set? Move to Quick Start Guide to run your first gene prediction!"

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: 这是为了回应审稿人 #5 关于 502 错误的质疑（教他们如何在本地跑起来）：

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start Guide
docs.text: This guide will walk you through the standard workflow of GENIUS-LLM, from downloading the source code to generating your first gene function prediction.

docs.title: 1. Download the Project
docs.text:
Use git to clone the repository directly from GitHub. This ensures you have the latest version of the framework.

# Clone the repository
git clone [https://github.com/yimengzhiyan/GENIUS-LLM.git](https://github.com/yimengzhiyan/GENIUS-LLM.git)

# Enter the project directory
cd GENIUS-LLM

docs.title: 2. Fast Configuration
docs.text:
Before running, you must provide your API credentials. GENIUS-LLM is designed to be provider-agnostic.

Edit config/data_config.yaml: 

model:
    url: https://api.openai.com/v1 # 这里展示API 的基准地址，你可根据自己的情况修改
    api_key: sk-proj-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx # replace your api_key
    model: The model you want to use # 你使用的模型名称

docs.title: 3. The Three-Step Workflow
docs.text:
GENIUS-LLM simplifies complex bioinformatics pipelines into three standardized commands.

Step 1: Import Knowledge Base First, populate your local MongoDB with gene annotation data. 

genius-import --input ./data/example_annotations.csv
Step 2: Pre-process Raw Data Clean and format your input data for LLM compatibility. 
genius-process --input ./data/raw_samples.xlsx --output ./data/cleaned_data.csv

Step 3: Execute Prediction Run the intelligent prediction engine to infer gene functions. 

genius-predict

docs.title: 4. Expected Output
docs.text:
After running genius-predict, you should see logs indicating the progress. The final results will be saved in the directory defined in your config (default: predict_results/).

Sample Log Output(以deepseek输出结果为例): 

--- GENIUS-LLM Prediction System ---
2026-04-23 18:51:42,984 - INFO - Starting prediction for Gene ID: AT1G03000
2026-04-23 18:51:48,205 - INFO - Prompt successfully generated.
2026-04-23 18:51:49,612 - INFO - HTTP Request: POST https://api.deepseek.com/chat/completions "HTTP/1.1 200 OK"
2026-04-23 18:52:06,390 - INFO - Model response received.

=== Prediction Result ===
            *
            *
            *
2026-04-23 18:52:06,391 - INFO - Task completed successfully.
2026-04-23 18:52:06,391 - INFO - Prediction result successfully saved to predict_results\AT1G03000_prediction.txt

docs.title: ⚙️ Configuration Guide
docs.text: GENIUS-LLM uses a centralized YAML configuration system to manage paths, models, and processing logic.

docs.title: 1. Project Structure
docs.text:
To ensure the automated path detection (GPS Logic) works correctly, please maintain the following structure: 

GENIUS-LLM/
├── config/
│   └── data_config.yaml    # Main configuration
├── data/                   # Input and processed data
├── src/                    # Source code
└── setup.py

docs.title: 2. YAML Field Explanations
docs.text: Edit config/data_config.yaml to suit your research:

docs.title: Model Settings:
docs.text:
api_key: Your LLM service provider key.
url: API endpoint (Default: DeepSeek).
model: Model ID (e.g., deepseek-chat, gpt-4o).

docs.title: Mongodb Settings:
docs.text:
mongodb:
  uri: "mongodb://localhost:27017/"
  database: "arabidopsis_gene_db" # Database names for corresponding species (need to be changed)

docs.title: Data Paths(process_data):
docs.text:
You can define multiple data sources here. The type determines which Processor (Blast/TWAS) is used. 

data_paths:
  blast:
    input: "data/yourdata_process/01gene_blast.txt"
    output: "data/yourdata_process_output/blast_results_text.txt"
  coexpression:
    input: "data/yourdata_process/02cultivar"
    output: "data/yourdata_process_output/coexpression_output.txt"

docs.title: Data Paths(import_data):
docs.text:
data_imports:
  blast:
    input_path: "data/yourdata_process_output/blast_results_text.txt"  # Read the gene alignment data from the file "data/test_import/01gene_blast_cleaned.txt" and import it into the MongoDB collection.
    collection: "gene_blast_similarity"
  coexpression:
    input_path: "data/yourdata_process_output/coexpression_output.txt"
    collection: "gene_coexpression"

docs.title: 📊 Compatibility Matrix & LLM Support
docs.text: Reviewer #4 noted the use of the OpenAI client in our codebase. We would like to clarify that GENIUS-LLM adopts a Protocol-Oriented Architecture. We utilize the OpenAI Python library not as a provider-specific lock-in, but as a universal adapter for the industry-standard completion protocol.

docs.title: 1. Supported Models Matrix
docs.text:
The following table explicitly lists the models verified with the current release of GENIUS-LLM. Compatibility is achieved by simply updating the url and model fields in data_config.yaml.

Model Category Representative Models Protocol Status Support Level OpenAI Series GPT-4o, GPT-4-turbo, GPT-3.5 Native ✅ Verified DeepSeek Series DeepSeek-V3, DeepSeek-Chat OpenAI-Compatible ✅ Verified Local LLMs Llama 3, Qwen, Mistral via vLLM / Ollama ✅ Verified National LLMs Moonshot (Kimi), Zhipu AI OpenAI-Compatible ✅ Verified Other Protocols Claude, Gemini Native SDK 🔄 via Proxy*

Note on Claude/Gemini: To maintain a lightweight codebase without dependency bloat, native SDKs for Anthropic/Google are not included. However, users can access these models via API gateways (e.g., One-API or LiteLLM) that convert their requests into the standard OpenAI format.

docs.title: 2. Decoupled Architecture
docs.text:
Our src/model/llm_model.py is designed with zero hardcoded endpoints. The internal logic treats the LLM as a black-box service defined by three parameters in the configuration:

  1. api_key: User-provided credential.
  2. url: The entry point for the API (e.g., https://api.deepseek.com/v1).
  3. model: The specific model identifier.

docs.title: Why use the openai library?
docs.text: The openai Python client has evolved into a standardized wrapper for RESTful LLM APIs. By leveraging this library, GENIUS-LLM benefits from: * Robust Streaming: Stable handling of long-context gene function descriptions. * Automatic Retries: Built-in exponential backoff for scientific computing stability. * Format Consistency: Ensures that the JSON output from different providers (DeepSeek vs. GPT) is parsed identically.

docs.title: 3. How to Switch Models (Step-by-Step)
docs.text: To switch from the default configuration to a different provider, modify your config/data_config.yaml as follows:

docs.title: Example: Switching to a Local Llama-3 (via vLLM)
docs.text:
model:
  api_key: "not-needed-for-local"
  url: "http://localhost:8000/v1"
  model: "meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct"
